title: English Literature, Essay Composition & Analysis
subject: English Language Arts & Literature
description: Master persuasive essay writing, literary thesis development, rhetorical analysis, and technical document drafting.
learningObjectives: Formulate a clear, arguable thesis statement supported by textual evidence.
Apply rhetorical strategies (Ethos, Pathos, Logos) in persuasive writing.
Cite sources accurately using standard academic citation styles.

lessons.title: Formulating a Strong Thesis Statement
lessons.content: A thesis statement is the central claim of your essay. It must be specific, arguable, and supported by logical evidence throughout your body paragraphs.

lessons.title: Rhetorical Appeals: Ethos, Pathos & Logos
lessons.content: ETHOS appeals to credibility and character. LOGOS appeals to logic, data, and reason. PATHOS appeals to emotion and human experience. Effective persuasive writing balances all three.

flashcards.definition: A single sentence that summarizes the main claim or argument of an essay.
An appeal to credibility, ethics, or moral authority.
An appeal to logic, evidence, statistics, and rational argument.
An appeal to emotion and empathy.

questions.question: Which rhetorical appeal relies on logical data, statistics, and verifiable evidence?
Where is the thesis statement typically placed in a standard 5-paragraph academic essay?
options: At the very end of the introduction paragraph

practicalChallenge.title: Persuasive Essay Outline
practicalChallenge.instruction: Draft a 1-page persuasive essay on an important homestead or community topic. Include a clear Thesis Statement and 3 supporting arguments!
